LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THE DEFECT IS TRIVIAL. THE COURT RELIES ON DEFENDANT'S FACTS 10-14, 16, 20-34, AND EXS. A, B & C TO THE RADELL DECLARATION. THE WEXLER DECLARATION DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT FOR HE DOES NOT ADRESS THE CAUSES OF THE ACCIDENT, PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO LOOK. THE MOTION BASED ON DESIGN IMMUNITY IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. THE COURT HAS SUSTAINED PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, AND 9 AND THE IDENTICAL OBJECTIONS TO THE SAME EVIDENCE RELATED TO OTHER PARTS OF THE MOTION. AS A CONSEQUENCE, DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE RECORDS RELIED UP BY DECLARANTS GOLDEN AND RADELL ARE ADMISSIBLE AS AN EXCEPTION TO THE HEARSAY RULE. THE FOUNDATIONAL FACTS REQUIRED BY EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1280 HAVE NOT BEEN SHOWN. AS TO GOLDEN, THERE ARE INSUFFICIENT FACTS TO PROVIDE A FOUNDATION FOR HIS TESTIMONY REGARDING EVENTS THAT OCCURRED BEFORE GOLDEN BECAME AN EMPLOYEE. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER ON MOTION. COURT RULED ON BOTH PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, BOTH ORDERS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
REMOVED FROM SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE CALENDAR SET FOR OCT-27-2008 - NO APPEARANCE. (210)
POS ON REPLY OF DEFT'S OPPOS. TO MTN FOR ORDER AWARDING DEFENSE COSTS AND DEC. OF MARK HOSTETTER FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION AWARDING DEFENSE COSTS, INCLUDING ATTORNEY AND WITNESS FEES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FALL IS ATTRIBUTABLE SOLIDLY AND EXCLUSIVELY TO HER ADMITTEDLY NOT LOOKING WHERE SHE WAS GOING. HER VISION OF THE TWO STEPS WAS UNOBSTRUCTED. THE TWO STEPS WERE OPEN AND OBVIOUS AND WERE THE VERY STAIRS PLAINTIFF HAD ASCENDED GOING INTO THE BUILDING. NO ONE COULD REASONABLY BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF HAD A VALID CLAIM BASED ON THE UNDISPUTED FACTS. PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 1038(A), THE COURT AWARDS DEFENDANT ITS ATTORNEYS FEES IN THE SUM OF $41,236 AND COSTS $894 FOR DEPOSITIONS, $1,286 FOR SUBPOENA COSTS, $3,620 FOR EXPERT COSTS AND COPYING COSTS OF $5,489. ALTHOUGH CCP SECTION 1038(B) DOES NOT SPECIFY COPYING COSTS, THE BREADTH OF CCP SECTION 1038(A) MAKES IT REASONABLE TO AWARD SUCH COSTS IN THIS CASE FOR THE TECHNICAL DRAWINGS MAKE CLEAR THE LIMITATIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF SARAH STROTHER'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS OFF CALENDAR BASED ON THE RULING IN THE RELATED MATTER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Dec-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Louis Kelly, Esq. of Kirtland & Packard, 310-536-1000 for plaintiff and opposing party Sarah Strother

Ruling:
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S Motion Awarding Defense Costs, Including Attorney And Witness Fees. The motion is granted. Plaintiff's fall is attributable solidly and exclusively to her admittedly not looking where she was going. Her vision of the two steps was unobstructed. The two steps were open and obvious and were the very stairs plaintiff had ascended going into the building. No one could reasonably believe that plaintiff had a valid claim based on the undisputed facts. Pursuant to CCP section 1038(a), the Court awards defendant its attorneys fees in the sum of $41,236 and costs $894 for depositions, $1,286 for subpoena costs, $3,620 for expert costs and copying costs of $5,489. Although CCP section 1038(b) does not specify copying costs, the breadth of CCP Section 1038(a) makes it reasonable to award such costs in this case for the technical drawings make clear the limitations of plaintiff's claim. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/PJM/PB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Michael L. Kelly, Esq. of Kirtland & Packard LLP 310/536-1000 & Michael W. Milward, Esq. of Law Office of Michael W. Milward and Ellen D. Vogt 209/795-0271 for pltf & opposing party; Mark W. Hostetter, Esq. of Vogl & Meredith LLP 398-0200 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - The motion is granted. The Court finds that, as a matter of law, the defect is trivial. The Court relies on defendant's facts 10-14, 16, 20-34, and Exs. A, B & C to the Radell declaration. The Wexler declaration does not create a triable issue of fact for he does not adress the causes of the accident, plaintiff's failure to look. The motion based on design immunity is denied for failure to shift the burden of producing evidence. The Court has sustained plaintiff's objections 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, and 9 and the identical objections to the same evidence related to other parts of the motion. As a consequence, defendant has not shown that the records relied up by declarants Golden and Radell are admissible as an exception to the hearsay rule. The foundational facts required by Evidence Code section 1280 have not been shown. As to Golden, there are insufficient facts to provide a foundation for his testimony regarding events that occurred before Golden became an employee. Defendant to submit proposed form of order on motion. Court ruled on both plaintiff's and defendant's objections to evidence, both orders signed in open court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
